description: Launch-ready polish stack for connections-helper. Implements items #4, #5, #6, #8, #9 from the /ro:app-polish checklist: dynamic OG images, PWA install, SEO (sitemap + JSON-LD), accessibility CI, and visual regression. Each story is runnable in one ralph iteration. Reference skills: /ro:og-image-dynamic, /ro:pwa-install, /ro:seo-launch-ready, /ro:accessibility-ci, /ro:visual-regression.

userStories.title: SEO: robots.txt + dynamic sitemap route
userStories.description: As a search engine crawler, I want a robots.txt that points to a sitemap, and a sitemap that lists the homepage, /how-it-works, and the last 365 puzzle dates, so I can index the app thoroughly.
acceptanceCriteria: public/robots.txt exists with Sitemap: line pointing to the production URL
src/routes/sitemap[.]xml.ts exists and returns valid XML
Sitemap includes homepage, /how-it-works, and the last 365 puzzle dates as /?date=YYYY-MM-DD
Response has Content-Type: application/xml and 1-day edge cache
Local curl of /sitemap.xml returns 200 with valid urlset XML
userStories.notes: Route tree confirms /sitemap.xml registered. Build passes. 369 URLs (4 static + 365 daily puzzles).

userStories.title: SEO: JSON-LD WebApplication schema + canonical + full meta set
userStories.description: As a social crawler (Twitter, LinkedIn, Discord) or Google's rich results engine, I want a WebApplication JSON-LD block, canonical URLs per route, and complete OG+Twitter meta so previews unfurl correctly and Google can surface rich results.
acceptanceCriteria: src/routes/__root.tsx includes JSON-LD WebApplication schema in scripts
Root route sets og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url, og:type, og:site_name, twitter:card=summary_large_image, twitter:image
Canonical link set in __root.tsx pointing to the production origin + current path
how-it-works route overrides title + description with route-specific copy
Rich Results Test passes against the built site (manual verification note in progress file)
userStories.notes: Added SITE_ORIGIN + OG_IMAGE constants, JSON-LD WebApplication in scripts[], og:url/og:site_name/canonical in __root. how-it-works has a head() override with route-specific title+description+canonical.

userStories.title: PWA: manifest.webmanifest + icons + meta + SW registration
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want to install the app to my home screen so I can open it with one tap instead of navigating a browser.
acceptanceCriteria: public/manifest.webmanifest exists with name, short_name, description, start_url=/?source=pwa, display=standalone, theme_color, icons (192, 512, maskable-512)
public/icons/icon-192.png and icon-512.png and icon-maskable-512.png exist (generated from existing favicon or logo)
src/routes/__root.tsx links manifest and adds theme-color, apple-mobile-web-app-capable meta
Client entry registers /sw.js only in PROD when serviceWorker is available
DevTools Application tab shows no manifest warnings (manual verification note in progress file)
userStories.notes: manifest.webmanifest + 3 icons (192, 512, maskable-512) generated from favicon.svg. __root.tsx links manifest + apple-touch-icon + adds mobile-web-app-capable meta. SW registration added to App.tsx — PROD + serviceWorker guarded, registers /sw.js on window.load.

userStories.title: PWA: service worker with network-first HTML + cache-first assets
userStories.description: As a mobile user who taps the app icon while briefly offline, I want the last-cached page to appear instantly (with an offline fallback if needed) instead of Chrome's dino.
acceptanceCriteria: public/sw.js exists with install/activate/fetch handlers
HTML requests (request.mode='navigate' or accept includes text/html) use network-first with cache fallback
Static assets (.js .css .woff2 .png .svg etc) use cache-first
API requests are NOT cached by the SW
CACHE const uses a versioned name (e.g. 'conn-v1') that can be bumped on deploy
Activate handler deletes any cache whose key !== CACHE
userStories.notes: public/sw.js with CACHE='conn-v1', install precaches /, activate deletes stale caches, fetch handler is network-first for HTML with fallback to /, cache-first for static assets, pass-through for /api/ requests. eslint ignore added for public/**.

userStories.title: PWA: InstallPrompt component with dismiss + session guard
userStories.description: As a returning visitor (not first visit), I want an unobtrusive install prompt with a clear dismiss action that doesn't nag me again.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/InstallPrompt.tsx exists
Captures beforeinstallprompt event (preventDefault + store)
Does NOT render on first visit — wait for sessionStorage visit-count >= 2
Dismiss stores flag in localStorage; does not re-prompt after dismiss
Fires track('cta_clicked', { cta: 'pwa_install', location: 'prompt:accepted' | 'prompt:dismissed' })
Dialog uses role='dialog' with aria-label='Install this app'
Rendered in __root.tsx layout
userStories.notes: src/components/InstallPrompt.tsx renders a role='dialog' banner when beforeinstallprompt fires AND sessionStorage visit-count >= 2. Dismiss writes localStorage flag (permanent); track() fires cta_clicked with location prompt:accepted|prompt:dismissed. Rendered in App.tsx inside TooltipProvider. Added cta_clicked to EventPayloads in src/lib/posthog.ts.

userStories.title: a11y: vitest-axe setup + component-level accessibility tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want axe violations to fail unit tests on the most-important components so a11y regressions are caught before they reach CI.
acceptanceCriteria: vitest-axe + axe-core + @testing-library/react installed as devDeps
src/test/axe-helper.ts exports expectNoA11yViolations(container)
Axe tests exist for: ShareButton, SettingsDialog (or settings route render), HintsPanel (or equivalent), StatsBanner
pnpm test passes with zero axe violations on the covered components
Tests use expect.extend(toHaveNoViolations) setup in a global test setup file

userStories.title: a11y: @axe-core/playwright e2e tests on key routes
userStories.description: As a user with assistive tech, I want every rendered route to pass WCAG 2 AA checks at the e2e level so missed a11y bugs in composition (not just components) are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: @axe-core/playwright installed as devDep
e2e/accessibility.spec.ts exists
Tests cover /, /how-it-works, /settings, /design-system (tags: wcag2a, wcag2aa, wcag21aa)
Tests fail when violations array is non-empty
pnpm exec playwright test e2e/accessibility.spec.ts passes locally

userStories.title: a11y: Lighthouse CI with 0.95 accessibility threshold
userStories.description: As the team, we want a per-PR Lighthouse accessibility score of >= 0.95 as a build gate so the app doesn't silently regress.
acceptanceCriteria: @lhci/cli installed as devDep
lighthouserc.json exists with startServerCommand=pnpm preview, url=[/, /how-it-works], numberOfRuns=3
Assertions: categories:accessibility >= 0.95 (error), best-practices >= 0.9 (warn), seo >= 0.9 (warn), performance >= 0.8 (warn)
.github/workflows/lighthouse.yml runs lhci on PR
Local pnpm exec lhci autorun passes against a built app

userStories.title: a11y: aria-live regions + focus management + skip-to-content
userStories.description: As a screen-reader user, I want async state changes announced, modal focus managed, and a skip link so I can navigate the app without a mouse.
acceptanceCriteria: Definitions loading region has role='status' aria-live='polite' aria-busy={isLoading}
Any error rendering uses role='alert'
ShareButton and SettingsDialog (or equivalent) focus the first interactive element on open and return focus to the trigger on close
Skip-to-content link is the first focusable element in __root.tsx, visible only when focused
<main id='main-content' tabIndex={-1}> wraps the main content area

userStories.title: Dynamic OG: /api/og endpoint via Satori + resvg-wasm on Workers
userStories.description: As someone sharing a puzzle-day link, I want the preview card to show the puzzle date + a branded card so the share looks intentional, not generic.
acceptanceCriteria: satori + @resvg/resvg-wasm installed
src/routes/api/og.ts exists and returns image/png
Accepts ?date=YYYY-MM-DD and ?title=... query params
Uses Inter (or existing app font) bundled via Vite; no runtime Google Fonts fetches
Returns 1200x630 PNG with Cache-Control: public, max-age=86400, s-maxage=604800, immutable
Falls back to a default card if params missing
curl 'http://localhost:3000/api/og?date=2026-04-12' returns valid PNG (file size > 10kb)

userStories.title: Dynamic OG: wire og:image per route to /api/og
userStories.description: As a social-media user, I want each shareable URL to unfurl with its own OG image so my shares stand out in a feed.
acceptanceCriteria: Root route og:image and twitter:image point to /api/og?title=Connections+Helper
Index route (with ?date= param) sets og:image to /api/og?date=<date>&title=Connections+for+<date>
how-it-works route sets og:image to /api/og?title=How+it+works
Removed references to static /og-image.png where they are replaced by dynamic URLs
Absolute URLs used for og:image (https://origin + path) so crawlers can fetch them

userStories.title: Visual regression: Playwright toHaveScreenshot baselines
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want pixel-diff baselines for the key routes across mobile + desktop so CSS regressions are caught before merge.
acceptanceCriteria: playwright.config.ts includes expect.toHaveScreenshot defaults (maxDiffPixelRatio=0.01, threshold=0.2, animations=disabled, caret=hide)
use block includes locale=en-GB, timezoneId=Europe/Amsterdam, colorScheme=light, reducedMotion=reduce
e2e/visual.spec.ts covers /, /how-it-works, /?date=2026-04-12 at mobile (390x844) and desktop (1440x900)
Test masks anything dynamic (stats counters, timestamps) via mask: [locator]
Baselines committed under e2e/__screenshots__ (captured via Linux docker for CI consistency OR noted as macOS-baseline with a TODO)

userStories.title: Visual regression: GitHub Actions workflow with diff artifact
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want failed visual-regression runs to upload the diff PNGs and comment on the PR so I can see at a glance what changed.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/visual-regression.yml exists
Runs on pull_request, installs deps, builds app, runs e2e/visual.spec.ts
On failure, uploads test-results/ as artifact with 7-day retention
On failure, posts a PR comment pointing at the artifact and the --update-snapshots command
Uses mcr.microsoft.com/playwright container OR pnpm exec playwright install --with-deps chromium
